article_title: Anthropic Buys All of SpaceX's Colossus 1 Capacity in Memphis, Adding 300 Megawatts and 220,000 GPUs to Claude's Inference Fleet
summary: Duplicate of already-published article on the Anthropic/SpaceX Colossus 1 deal (PR #1192, merged earlier in this batch). The work is discarded under v3.9.0; not eligible for rewrite.

findings.details: Already-published article: src/content/articles/2026-05/08-anthropic-leases-the-full-capacity-of-spacexs-colossus-1-data-center-adding-300-mw-and-over-220000-nvidia-gpus-to-the-claude-backend.md (originally PR #1192). Same Anthropic/SpaceX deal, same Colossus 1 / Memphis facility, same 300 MW / 220,000 GPU figures, same May 6 2026 announcement.

content_preview.title: Anthropic Buys All of SpaceX's Colossus 1 Capacity in Memphis, Adding 300 Megawatts and 220,000 GPUs to Claude's Inference Fleet
content_preview.summary: The deal between former rivals gives Anthropic the entire 300 MW Memphis supercomputer that xAI built in 2024, immediately doubling Claude Code rate limits and removing peak-hours throttling.
content_preview.body_excerpt:
## Overview

Anthropic and SpaceX announced on May 6 that Anthropic will take over the entire compute capacity of Colossus 1, the Memphis-area supercomputer originally built by xAI in 2024. According to [Anthropic](https://www.anthropic.com/news/higher-limits-spacex), the agreement gives Claude "more than 300 megawatts of new capacity (over 220,000 NVIDIA GPUs) within the month," and the company is using the additional resources to immediately raise customer-facing limits. The deal puts a former...

editor_notes.content_quality: Substantively well-written. Uses different source set (anthropic.com/news + TechCrunch + SiliconANGLE + Fortune) than PR #1192 (anthropic.com/news + Tom's Hardware + Al Jazeera + Yahoo Finance), but covers the same disclosure event with substantively identical facts.
editor_notes.factual_accuracy: Same facts as the already-published article — same 300 MW / 220,000 NVIDIA GPU lease, same rate-limit changes, same orbital-compute MOU.
recommendations: Bot operations: parallel write-article agents need topic-collision detection that includes in-flight submissions.
editor_notes.overall_assessment: REJECT under v3.9.0. PR #1192 covers this disclosure.
